entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop being studied for grain quality and malting potential

entities.name: GRAIN QUALITY
entities.description: The overall quality characteristics of the sorghum grain

entities.name: MALTING POTENTIAL
entities.description: The capacity of sorghum grain to be converted into malt

entities.name: GRAIN AND MALT QUALITY TESTS
entities.description: Analytical tests used to evaluate the quality of sorghum grain and malt

entities.name: WHITE PERICARP
entities.description: A trait where the sorghum grain has a white outer layer

entities.name: YELLOW PERICARP
entities.description: A trait where the sorghum grain has a yellow outer layer

entities.name: BROWN GRAIN
entities.description: A trait where the sorghum grain is brown

entities.name: MILLING ENERGY
entities.description: The energy required to mill the sorghum grain

entities.name: EXTRACT
entities.description: The amount of soluble material obtained from the grain

entities.name: GRAIN NITROGEN CONTENT
entities.description: The total nitrogen present in the sorghum grain

entities.name: MALTING
entities.description: The process of germinating and drying grain to produce malt

entities.name: NITROGEN EXTRACTED
entities.description: The amount of nitrogen released during the malting process

entities.name: SOLUBLE NITROGEN RATIO
entities.description: The proportion of nitrogen that is soluble

entities.name: MALT MILLING ENERGY
entities.description: The energy required to mill the malted sorghum

entities.name: GRINDING RESISTANCE
entities.description: The resistance of the grain to grinding during milling

entities.name: STARCH DAMAGE
entities.description: The physical disruption of starch granules during the milling process

entities.name: MILLING
entities.description: The process of grinding the grain

entities.name: SOLUBILISATION OF STARCH
entities.description: The process by which starch becomes soluble

entities.name: ENDOSPERM MODIFICATION
entities.description: The biochemical breakdown of the endosperm during malting

entities.name: GELATINISATION TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature at which unmodified starch granules gelatinize

relationships.description: Sorghum is evaluated for grain quality
Sorghum is evaluated for malting potential
Tests were applied to sorghum cultivars
Sorghum cultivars can have white pericarp
Sorghum cultivars can have yellow pericarp
Sorghum cultivars can have brown grain
Milling energy is related to extract in white and yellow sorghums
Milling energy is related to grain nitrogen content in white and yellow sorghums
Malting correlates with the amount of nitrogen extracted
Close relationship between soluble nitrogen ratio and malt milling energy
High levels of extract are associated with higher grinding resistance
Starch damage facilitates the solubilisation of starch
Solubilisation of starch overcomes poor endosperm modification
Solubilisation of starch overcomes high gelatinisation temperature
